Top Stories Following The Verge The Verge logo. The MacBook Neo is the best thing to happen to Windows in years Microsoft always responds to a threat to Windows by improving the operating system. Tom Warren Apr 9 Top Stories 1 Play The AI industry’s race for profits is now existential Nilay Patel Apr 9 Play The AI industry’s race for profits is now existential Nilay Patel Apr 9 2 A jury is about to decide the fate of Ticketmaster Lauren Feiner Apr 9 A jury is about to decide the fate of Ticketmaster Lauren Feiner Apr 9 3 I tested three Windows laptops in the MacBook Neo’s price range — there’s no contest Antonio G. Di Benedetto Apr 9 I tested three Windows laptops in the MacBook Neo’s price range — there’s no contest Antonio G. Di Benedetto Apr 9 4 I finally get the iPhone Air Allison Johnson Apr 8 I finally get the iPhone Air Allison Johnson Apr 8 5 The Exit 8 movie is even better if you play the game first Andrew Webster Apr 9 The Exit 8 movie is even better if you play the game first Andrew Webster Apr 9 Today’s Stream Following Today’s Storystream Feed refreshed 20 minutes ago Honey, Dyson shrunk its fans. Dominic Preston 20 minutes ago Android’s “tap to share” looks a little different to Apple’s. Last month Android Authority reported Google is developing an NFC-based way to share contact info, files, links, and more, and now it’s got the interface running . Unlike Apple’s NameDrop, users have to hold the two phones above one another, perhaps to account for how varied NFC chip placement can be on Android devices. Previous Next 1/2 While iPhone users hold their phones edge to edge, on Android the phones must overlap. Image: Android Authority Link Thomas Ricker An hour ago Lame loses his sparkle. TikTok star Khaby Lame sold his personal brand to a small, relatively unknown company called “Rich Sparkle Holdings.” Instead of handing him $975 million in cash, they paid him in stock. Fans piled in, causing the price to skyrocket, briefly making Lame a paper billionaire (several times over) before the price plummeted. It’s now looking suspiciously like a “pump-and-dump” scheme, causing trading apps to freeze the stock. 🤷‍♂️ TikTok's top star teased a $975 million deal and then went silent. Red flags are piling up. [Business Insider] Link Thomas Ricker Two hours ago Claude Cowork is ready to take over your company. Anthropic’s shared, agentic AI workspace for macOS and Windows is getting much-needed tools for IT admins to do company-wide deployments, letting anyone build and deploy autonomous workflows so long as their organization is on a paid plan. It now adds the ability to turn Zoom meeting transcripts into action items. Link Advertiser Content From This is the title for the native ad Thomas Ricker Two hours ago Apple to close three retail stores, including the first to unionize. The stores are all located in struggling shopping malls . Employees at the non-union shops can transfer to nearby stores, but union workers from Towson, Maryland can apply for open roles. Apple says this is due to their collective bargain agreement, which the union disputes, threatening legal action. Apple is Permanently Closing Three U.S. Stores in June [MacRumors] Link Richard Lawler 12:07 AM UTC Meta removes ads from lawyers seeking plaintiffs for social media addiction cases. Now that a jury has ruled against Meta and YouTube in a landmark trial, the sharks are circling , and what better place to find potential clients than on those social media platforms ? Link Richard Lawler Apr 9 How can you help NASA astronauts land on the Moon? You might not be able to fly on an Artemis mission , but you could help them navigate transitions across the gravity on Earth, in transit, and on the Moon. Navy researchers are seeking volunteers to help find out “how the brain and inner ear respond to motion and to space motion sickness mitigation techniques” using an Air Force centrifuge: Participants will complete up to eight hours of testing across two days, including exposure to three times the force of Earth’s gravity acceleration profile inside the centrifuge, and a series of balance and vision assessments. The Federal Trade Commission accused StubHub of failing to show customers the full cost of tickets upfront, and the settlement would require clearer disclosure. Microsoft starts removing Copilot buttons from Windows 11 apps Tom Warren An hour ago Dominic Preston Two hours ago Android’s “tap to share” looks a little different to Apple’s. Last month Android Authority reported Google is developing an NFC-based way to share contact info, files, links, and more, and now it’s got the interface running . Unlike Apple’s NameDrop, users have to hold the two phones above one another, perhaps to account for how varied NFC chip placement can be on Android devices. Previous Next 1/2 While iPhone users hold their phones edge to edge, on Android the phones must overlap. Image: Android Authority Link Thomas Ricker Two hours ago Lame loses his sparkle. TikTok star Khaby Lame sold his personal brand to a small, relatively unknown company called “Rich Sparkle Holdings.” Instead of handing him $975 million in cash, they paid him in stock. Fans piled in, causing the price to skyrocket, briefly making Lame a paper billionaire (several times over) before the price plummeted. It’s now looking suspiciously like a “pump-and-dump” scheme, causing trading apps to freeze the stock. 🤷‍♂️ TikTok's top star teased a $975 million deal and then went silent. Red flags are piling up. [Business Insider] Link Advertiser Content From This is the title for the native ad Thomas Ricker 7:15 AM UTC Claude Cowork is ready to take over your company. Anthropic’s shared, agentic AI workspace for macOS and Windows is getting much-needed tools for IT admins to do company-wide deployments, letting anyone build and deploy autonomous workflows so long as their organization is on a paid plan. It now adds the ability to turn Zoom meeting transcripts into action items. Link Thomas Ricker 6:45 AM UTC Apple to close three retail stores, including the first to unionize. The stores are all located in struggling shopping malls . Employees at the non-union shops can transfer to nearby stores, but union workers from Towson, Maryland can apply for open roles. Apple says this is due to their collective bargain agreement, which the union disputes, threatening legal action. Microsoft starts removing Copilot buttons from Windows 11 apps Tom Warren An hour ago Dominic Preston Two hours ago Android’s “tap to share” looks a little different to Apple’s. Last month Android Authority reported Google is developing an NFC-based way to share contact info, files, links, and more, and now it’s got the interface running . Unlike Apple’s NameDrop, users have to hold the two phones above one another, perhaps to account for how varied NFC chip placement can be on Android devices. Previous Next 1/2 While iPhone users hold their phones edge to edge, on Android the phones must overlap. Image: Android Authority Link Thomas Ricker Two hours ago Lame loses his sparkle. TikTok star Khaby Lame sold his personal brand to a small, relatively unknown company called “Rich Sparkle Holdings.” Instead of handing him $975 million in cash, they paid him in stock. Fans piled in, causing the price to skyrocket, briefly making Lame a paper billionaire (several times over) before the price plummeted. It’s now looking suspiciously like a “pump-and-dump” scheme, causing trading apps to freeze the stock. 🤷‍♂️ TikTok's top star teased a $975 million deal and then went silent. Red flags are piling up. [Business Insider] Link Advertiser Content From This is the title for the native ad Thomas Ricker 7:15 AM UTC Claude Cowork is ready to take over your company. Anthropic’s shared, agentic AI workspace for macOS and Windows is getting much-needed tools for IT admins to do company-wide deployments, letting anyone build and deploy autonomous workflows so long as their organization is on a paid plan. It now adds the ability to turn Zoom meeting transcripts into action items. Link Thomas Ricker 6:45 AM UTC Apple to close three retail stores, including the first to unionize. The stores are all located in struggling shopping malls . Employees at the non-union shops can transfer to nearby stores, but union workers from Towson, Maryland can apply for open roles. Apple says this is due to their collective bargain agreement, which the union disputes, threatening legal action. Apple is Permanently Closing Three U.S. Stores in June [MacRumors] Link Richard Lawler 12:07 AM UTC Meta removes ads from lawyers seeking plaintiffs for social media addiction cases. Now that a jury has ruled against Meta and YouTube in a landmark trial, the sharks are circling , and what better place to find potential clients than on those social media platforms ? The only problem is that Axios reports Meta pulled “more than a dozen” such ads from firms like Morgan & Morgan and Sokolove Law on Thursday. Link Richard Lawler Apr 9 How can you help NASA astronauts land on the Moon? You might not be able to fly on an Artemis mission , but you could help them navigate transitions across the gravity on Earth, in transit, and on the Moon. Navy researchers are seeking volunteers to help find out “how the brain and inner ear respond to motion and to space motion sickness mitigation techniques” using an Air Force centrifuge: Participants will complete up to eight hours of testing across two days, including exposure to three times the force of Earth’s gravity acceleration profile inside the centrifuge, and a series of balance and vision assessments. Link Lauren Feiner Apr 9 StubHub agrees to pay to $10 million to refund customers and settle deceptive pricing claims. As Ticketmaster wraps up its antitrust defense , a different ticketing company is in the government’s crosshairs. The Federal Trade Commission accused StubHub of failing to show customers the full cost of tickets upfront, and the settlement would require clearer disclosure. StubHub spokesperson Jack Sterne said the settlement “covers a limited number of transactions” across three days, and

Digital privacy non-profit the Electronic Frontier Foundation has announced it’s leaving X , citing a sharp decline in views for its posts. With EFF off the platform, some commenters agree with the organization’s thinking. Krankor : eff x, I agree Get the day’s best comment and more in my free newsletter, The Verge Daily . Link Advertiser Content From This is the title for the native ad Microsoft starts removing Copilot buttons from Windows 11 apps Tom Warren Two hours ago Dominic Preston Two hours ago Android’s “tap to share” looks a little different to Apple’s. Last month Android Authority reported Google is developing an NFC-based way to share contact info, files, links, and more, and now it’s got the interface running . Unlike Apple’s NameDrop, users have to hold the two phones above one another, perhaps to account for how varied NFC chip placement can be on Android devices. Previous Next 1/2 While iPhone users hold their phones edge to edge, on Android the phones must overlap. Image: Android Authority Link Thomas Ricker 7:48 AM UTC Lame loses his sparkle. TikTok star Khaby Lame sold his personal brand to a small, relatively unknown company called “Rich Sparkle Holdings.” Instead of handing him $975 million in cash, they paid him in stock. Fans piled in, causing the price to skyrocket, briefly making Lame a paper billionaire (several times over) before the price plummeted. It’s now looking suspiciously like a “pump-and-dump” scheme, causing trading apps to freeze the stock. 🤷‍♂️ TikTok's top star teased a $975 million deal and then went silent. Red flags are piling up. Di Benedetto Apr 9 2 The MacBook Neo is the best thing to happen to Windows in years Tom Warren Apr 9 3 Continuous glucose monitoring made me continuously crazy Victoria Song Apr 8 4 I finally get the iPhone Air Allison Johnson Apr 8 5 A jury is about to decide the fate of Ticketmaster Lauren Feiner Apr 9 Continuous glucose monitoring made me continuously crazy MAHA is obsessed with these wearables — for all the wrong reasons. Victoria Song Apr 8 Thomas Ricker 7:15 AM UTC Claude Cowork is ready to take over your company. Anthropic’s shared, agentic AI workspace for macOS and Windows is getting much-needed tools for IT admins to do company-wide deployments, letting anyone build and deploy autonomous workflows so long as their organization is on a paid plan. It now adds the ability to turn Zoom meeting transcripts into action items. Link Thomas Ricker 6:45 AM UTC Apple to close three retail stores, including the first to unionize. The stores are all located in struggling shopping malls . Employees at the non-union shops can transfer to nearby stores, but union workers from Towson, Maryland can apply for open roles. Apple says this is due to their collective bargain agreement, which the union disputes, threatening legal action. Apple is Permanently Closing Three U.S. Stores in June [MacRumors] Link Richard Lawler 12:07 AM UTC Meta removes ads from lawyers seeking plaintiffs for social media addiction cases. Now that a jury has ruled against Meta and YouTube in a landmark trial, the sharks are circling , and what better place to find potential clients than on those social media platforms ? The only problem is that Axios reports Meta pulled “more than a dozen” such ads from firms like Morgan & Morgan and Sokolove Law on Thursday. GizmoChina has images of the full line of Pura 90 Pro and Pro Max colors, but I’m a fan of this sunset Pro Max Huawei has teased on Weibo . Gradients aren’t new for Huawei — its P20 and P30 phones had similar finishes. Digital privacy non-profit the Electronic Frontier Foundation has announced it’s leaving X , citing a sharp decline in views for its posts. With EFF off the platform, some commenters agree with the organization’s thinking. Krankor : eff x, I agree Get the day’s best comment and more in my free newsletter, The Verge Daily . Victoria Song Apr 8 Dominic Preston 8:48 AM UTC Android’s “tap to share” looks a little different to Apple’s. Last month Android Authority reported Google is developing an NFC-based way to share contact info, files, links, and more, and now it’s got the interface running . Unlike Apple’s NameDrop, users have to hold the two phones above one another, perhaps to account for how varied NFC chip placement can be on Android devices. Previous Next 1/2 While iPhone users hold their phones edge to edge, on Android the phones must overlap. Image: Android Authority Link Thomas Ricker 7:48 AM UTC Lame loses his sparkle. TikTok star Khaby Lame sold his personal brand to a small, relatively unknown company called “Rich Sparkle Holdings.” Instead of handing him $975 million in cash, they paid him in stock. Fans piled in, causing the price to skyrocket, briefly making Lame a paper billionaire (several times over) before the price plummeted. It’s now looking suspiciously like a “pump-and-dump” scheme, causing trading apps to freeze the stock. 🤷‍♂️ TikTok's top star teased a $975 million deal and then went silent. Red flags are piling up. [Business Insider] Link Thomas Ricker 7:15 AM UTC Claude Cowork is ready to take over your company. Anthropic’s shared, agentic AI workspace for macOS and Windows is getting much-needed tools for IT admins to do company-wide deployments, letting anyone build and deploy autonomous workflows so long as their organization is on a paid plan. It now adds the ability to turn Zoom meeting transcripts into action items. Link Thomas Ricker 6:45 AM UTC Apple to close three retail stores, including the first to unionize. The stores are all located in struggling shopping malls . Employees at the non-union shops can transfer to nearby stores, but union workers from Towson, Maryland can apply for open roles. Apple says this is due to their collective bargain agreement, which the union disputes, threatening legal action. Apple is Permanently Closing Three U.S. Stores in June [MacRumors] Link Richard Lawler 12:07 AM UTC Meta removes ads from lawyers seeking plaintiffs for social media addiction cases. Now that a jury has ruled against Meta and YouTube in a landmark trial, the sharks are circling , and what better place to find potential clients than on those social media platforms ? The only problem is that Axios reports Meta pulled “more than a dozen” such ads from firms like Morgan & Morgan and Sokolove Law on Thursday. The Pura 90 series will launch in China on April 20th, and the phones will be decked out in a series of colorful gradient finishes. GizmoChina has images of the full line of Pura 90 Pro and Pro Max colors, but I’m a fan of this sunset Pro Max Huawei has teased on Weibo . Gradients aren’t new for Huawei — its P20 and P30 phones had similar finishes. Victoria Song Apr 8 Dominic Preston 8:48 AM UTC Android’s “tap to share” looks a little different to Apple’s. Last month Android Authority reported Google is developing an NFC-based way to share contact info, files, links, and more, and now it’s got the interface running . Unlike Apple’s NameDrop, users have to hold the two phones above one another, perhaps to account for how varied NFC chip placement can be on Android devices. Previous Next 1/2 While iPhone users hold their phones edge to edge, on Android the phones must overlap. Image: Android Authority Link Thomas Ricker 7:48 AM UTC Lame loses his sparkle. TikTok star Khaby Lame sold his personal brand to a small, relatively unknown company called “Rich Sparkle Holdings.” Instead of handing him $975 million in cash, they paid him in stock. Fans piled in, causing the price to skyrocket, briefly making Lame a paper billionaire (several times over) before the price plummeted. It’s now looking suspiciously like a “pump-and-dump” scheme, causing trading apps to freeze the stock. 🤷‍♂️ TikTok's top star teased a $975 million deal and then went silent. Red flags are piling up. [Business Insider] Link Thomas Ricker 7:15 AM UTC Claude Cowork is ready to take over your company. Anthropic’s shared, agentic AI workspace for macOS and Windows is getting much-needed tools for IT admins to do company-wide deployments, letting anyone build and deploy autonomous workflows so long as their organization is on a paid plan. It now adds the ability to turn Zoom meeting transcripts into action items. Link Thomas Ricker 6:45 AM UTC Apple to close three retail stores, including the first to unionize. The stores are all located in struggling shopping malls . Employees at the non-union shops can transfer to nearby stores, but union workers from Towson, Maryland can apply for open roles. Apple says this is due to their collective bargain agreement, which the union disputes, threatening legal action. Apple is Permanently Closing Three U.S. Stores in June [MacRumors] Link Richard Lawler 12:07 AM UTC Meta removes ads from lawyers seeking plaintiffs for social media addiction cases. Now that a jury has ruled against Meta and YouTube in a landmark trial, the sharks are circling , and what better place to find potential clients than on those social media platforms ? The only problem is that Axios reports Meta pulled “more than a dozen” such ads from firms like Morgan & Morgan and Sokolove Law on Thursday. France’s Interministerial Directorate for Digital Affairs (DINUM) has announced its “exit from Windows in favor of workstations running on the Linux operating system,” as XDA reports. It’s part of a broader shift away from non-European tech — in January, France’s government also launched its own videoconferencing platform to replace Teams, Zoom, and Google Meet. France's government is ditching Windows for Linux, calling US tech dependence a strategic risk [XDA] Link Emma Roth 14 minutes ago Prime Video’s pricier “Ultra” plan goes live today. Going ad-free on Prime Video now costs $4.99 per month, instead of $2.99. It also comes with “exclusive” access to 4K streams. Amazon Prime Video nearly doubles the price to go ad-free and stream 4K video Emma Roth Mar 13 Link Cloudflare made a WordPress for AI agents Emma Roth 43 minutes ago Advertiser Content From This is the title for the native ad Stevie Bonifield An hour ago Gmail’s end-to-end encryption is now available on its mobile app. Starting this week, enterprise users will be able to send encrypted messages from Gmail’s Android and iOS apps if their organization has the feature enabled. Gmail’s version of E2EE, which uses client-side encryption , has been available since last year , but is still limited to users with enterprise accounts. Gmail end-to-end encryption now available on mobile devices [Google Workspace Updates Blog] Link Dominic Preston Two hours ago Huawei’s next flagship phones are going big on gradients. Sign Up Modify Profile Podcasts Amadou Sy on Why Africa is Keeping its Debt Closer to Home March 12, 2026 Surging interest rates on dollar- and euro-denominated loans in recent years have prompted countries to turn to domestic markets for their borrowing needs. Amadou Sy heads the IMF Regional Studies division. In this podcast, he says there are benefits to issuing debt in local markets and in local currency. Johan Norberg on What Makes and Breaks Golden Eras February 5, 2026 The most prosperous of civilizations were the most open. The Romans, for example, expanded their empire by integrating the populations they conquered, creating a melting pot of new ideas. Johan Norberg’s latest book, Peak Human , examines the rise and fall of seven golden age civilizations. In this podcast, he says that if openness to learning and trade are what great societies make, building walls to protect their dominance often sparks their demise. Enrico Letta: Harnessing Europe's Single Market January 28, 2026 The idea of European integration through a single market was to capitalize on the EU as a whole. However, EU member states now appear reticent to pool sovereignty to the degree required to counter the geopolitical challenges of today’s world. Enrico Letta is President of the Jacques Delors Institute and a champion of Delors’ vision of creating a fully integrated European economic space. Letta says the adaptation of the single market should include all sectors rather than a select few and that fragmentation is holding Europe back. Georgina Torbet An hour ago Top Stories 1 Why your Whoop might tell you to up your testosterone Victoria Song 13 minutes ago Why your Whoop might tell you to up your testosterone Victoria Song 13 minutes ago 2 The MacBook Neo is the best thing to happen to Windows in years Tom Warren Apr 9 The MacBook Neo is the best thing to happen to Windows in years Tom Warren Apr 9 3 Fear and loathing at OpenAI David Pierce Two hours ago Fear and loathing at OpenAI David Pierce Two hours ago 4 I tested three Windows laptops in the MacBook Neo’s price range — there’s no contest Antonio G. Di Benedetto Apr 9 I tested three Windows laptops in the MacBook Neo’s price range — there’s no contest Antonio G. Di Benedetto Apr 9 5 Play The AI industry’s race for profits is now existential Nilay Patel Apr 9 Play The AI industry’s race for profits is now existential Nilay Patel Apr 9 Today’s Stream Following Today’s Storystream Feed refreshed 3 minutes ago Premium price hike Stevie Bonifield 3 minutes ago France’s government is switching to Linux. France’s Interministerial Directorate for Digital Affairs (DINUM) has announced its “exit from Windows in favor of workstations running on the Linux operating system,” as XDA reports. It’s part of a broader shift away from non-European tech — in January, France’s government also launched its own videoconferencing platform to replace Teams, Zoom, and Google Meet. France's government is ditching Windows for Linux, calling US tech dependence a strategic risk [XDA] Link Emma Roth 14 minutes ago Prime Video’s pricier “Ultra” plan goes live today. Going ad-free on Prime Video now costs $4.99 per month, instead of $2.99. It also comes with “exclusive” access to 4K streams. Amazon Prime Video nearly doubles the price to go ad-free and stream 4K video Emma Roth Mar 13 Link Cloudflare made a WordPress for AI agents Emma Roth 43 minutes ago Advertiser Content From This is the title for the native ad Stevie Bonifield An hour ago Gmail’s end-to-end encryption is now available on its mobile app. Starting this week, enterprise users will be able to send encrypted messages from Gmail’s Android and iOS apps if their organization has the feature enabled. Gmail’s version of E2EE, which uses client-side encryption , has been available since last year , but is still limited to users with enterprise accounts. Gmail end-to-end encryption now available on mobile devices [Google Workspace Updates Blog] Link Dominic Preston Two hours ago Huawei’s next flagship phones are going big on gradients. The Pura 90 series will launch in China on April 20th, and the phones will be decked out in a series of colorful gradient finishes. GizmoChina has images of the full line of Pura 90 Pro and Pro Max colors, but I’m a fan of this sunset Pro Max Huawei has teased on Weibo . Gradients aren’t new for Huawei — its P20 and P30 phones had similar finishes. Screenshot: Huawei Weibo account Link Snap is sticking with Qualcomm for its next AR glasses Jay Peters Two hours ago Most Popular Most Popular 1 I tested three Windows laptops in the MacBook Neo’s price range — there’s no contest Antonio G. Di Benedetto Apr 9 2 The MacBook Neo is the best thing to happen to Windows in years Tom Warren Apr 9 3 Continuous glucose monitoring made me continuously crazy Victoria Song Apr 8 4 A jury is about to decide the fate of Ticketmaster Lauren Feiner Apr 9 5 The AI industry’s race for profits is now existential Nilay Patel Apr 9 Continuous glucose monitoring made me continuously crazy MAHA is obsessed with these wearables — for all the wrong reasons. Victoria Song Apr 8 Gen Z’s love-hate relationship with AI Robert Hart Two hours ago YouTube Premium is getting pricier Dominic Preston Two hours ago Dominic Preston 11:00 AM UTC I see what you did there. Digital privacy non-profit the Electronic Frontier Foundation has announced it’s leaving X , citing a sharp decline in views for its posts. With EFF off the platform, some commenters agree with the organization’s thinking. Krankor : eff x, I agree Get the day’s best comment and more in my free newsletter, The Verge Daily . Link Microsoft starts removing Copilot buttons from Windows 11 apps Tom Warren 9:22 AM UTC Dominic Preston 8:48 AM UTC Android’s “tap to share” looks a little different to Apple’s. Last month Android Authority reported Google is developing an NFC-based way to share contact info, files, links, and more, and now it’s got the interface running . Unlike Apple’s NameDrop, users have to hold the two phones above one another, perhaps to account for how varied NFC chip placement can be on Android devices. Previous Next 1/2 While iPhone users hold their phones edge to edge, on Android the phones must overlap. Image: Android Authority Link Just For You Just For You 1 Amazon’s Starlink competitor Leo gets a new date Thomas Ricker Apr 9 2 I finally get the iPhone Air Allison Johnson Apr 8 3 Microsoft starts removing Copilot buttons from Windows 11 apps Tom Warren 9:22 AM UTC 4 The vibes are off at OpenAI Hayden Field Apr 8 5 Castlevania headlines a big list of exciting indie game reveals Jay Peters Apr 9 Sorry kid, drones are for war now The DJI ban was just the beginning. Sean Hollister Apr 7 Thomas Ricker 7:48 AM UTC Lame loses his sparkle. TikTok star Khaby Lame sold his personal brand to a small, relatively unknown company called “Rich Sparkle Holdings.” Instead of handing him $975 million in cash, they paid him in stock. Fans piled in, causing the price to skyrocket, briefly making Lame a paper billionaire (several times over) before the price plummeted. It’s now looking suspiciously like a “pump-and-dump” scheme, causing trading apps to freeze the stock. 🤷‍♂️ TikTok's top star teased a $975 million deal and then went silent. Red flags are piling up. [Business Insider] Link Thomas Ricker 7:15 AM UTC Claude Cowork is ready to take over your company. Anthropic’s shared, agentic AI workspace for macOS and Windows is getting much-needed tools for IT admins to do company-wide deployments, letting anyone build and deploy autonomous workflows so long as their organization is on a paid plan. It now adds the ability to turn Zoom meeting transcripts into action items. Link Thomas Ricker 6:45 AM UTC Apple to close three retail stores, including the first to unionize. The stores are all located in struggling shopping malls . Employees at the non-union shops can transfer to nearby stores, but union workers from Towson, Maryland can apply for open roles. Apple says this is due to their collective bargain agreement, which the union disputes, threatening lega

Gaby Del Valle 27 seconds ago Dominic Preston 24 minutes ago Samsung’s Z Flip 8 slims down in new leak renders. The fairly reliable leaker OnLeaks has shared his first CAD renders for the Galaxy Z Flip 8, via MyMobiles , and it looks… pretty much identical to the Z Flip 7 , actually. It’s apparently slimmer though, with 0.5mm shaved off when closed, which should make it fractionally more pocketable. Previous Next 1/3 The Flip 8 has the same boxy design and full cover screen as its predecessor according to this leak. Image: OnLeaks / MyMobiles Link Bose’s rugged Soundlink Flex Bluetooth speaker is on sale for up to $50 off Brandon Widder 28 minutes ago Advertiser Content From This is the title for the native ad Andrew Liszewski 31 minutes ago Adobe’s ‘computational photography’ camera app is now available for the iPad. After adding support for the iPhone 17 and its square sensor selfie camera last November, Adobe has updated its Project Indigo camera app to support the iPhone 17e and iPads with at least 6GB of RAM. But Adobe warns that “Project Indigo is not yet tuned for iPad operation” and suggests sticking with an iPhone for an “optimal experience.” Project Indigo App - App Store [App Store] Link Trump Mobile isn’t giving up just yet Dominic Preston 33 minutes ago Emma Roth 51 minutes ago Professor Layton is coming to PC and PlayStation for the first time. Arriving later this year, Professor Layton and the New World of Steam will be available on PlayStation 5 and Steam in addition to the Nintendo Switch and Switch 2. There’s still no firm release date for this puzzle-filled title just yet, but you can check out the latest trailer below. Victoria Song Apr 8 Stevie Bonifield Two hours ago France’s government is switching to Linux. France’s Interministerial Directorate for Digital Affairs (DINUM) has announced its “exit from Windows in favor of workstations running on the Linux operating system,” as XDA reports. It’s part of a broader shift away from non-European tech — in January, France’s government also launched its own videoconferencing platform to replace Teams, Zoom, and Google Meet. France's government is ditching Windows for Linux, calling US tech dependence a strategic risk [XDA] Link Emma Roth Two hours ago Prime Video’s pricier “Ultra” plan goes live today. Going ad-free on Prime Video now costs $4.99 per month, instead of $2.99. It also comes with “exclusive” access to 4K streams. Amazon Prime Video nearly doubles the price to go ad-free and stream 4K video Emma Roth Mar 13 Link Cloudflare made a WordPress for AI agents Emma Roth Two hours ago Stevie Bonifield Two hours ago Gmail’s end-to-end encryption is now available on its mobile app. Starting this week, enterprise users will be able to send encrypted messages from Gmail’s Android and iOS apps if their organization has the feature enabled. Gmail’s version of E2EE, which uses client-side encryption , has been available since last year , but is still limited to users with enterprise accounts. Gmail end-to-end encryption now available on mobile devices [Google Workspace Updates Blog] Link Dominic Preston 12:16 PM UTC Huawei’s next flagship phones are going big on gradients. The Pura 90 series will launch in China on April 20th, and the phones will be decked out in a series of colorful gradient finishes. GizmoChina has images of the full line of Pura 90 Pro and Pro Max colors, but I’m a fan of this sunset Pro Max Huawei has teased on Weibo . Gradients aren’t new for Huawei — its P20 and P30 phones had similar finishes. Gaby Del Valle 27 seconds ago Dominic Preston 24 minutes ago Samsung’s Z Flip 8 slims down in new leak renders. The fairly reliable leaker OnLeaks has shared his first CAD renders for the Galaxy Z Flip 8, via MyMobiles , and it looks… pretty much identical to the Z Flip 7 , actually. It’s apparently slimmer though, with 0.5mm shaved off when closed, which should make it fractionally more pocketable. Previous Next 1/3 The Flip 8 has the same boxy design and full cover screen as its predecessor according to this leak. Image: OnLeaks / MyMobiles Link Bose’s rugged Soundlink Flex Bluetooth speaker is on sale for up to $50 off Brandon Widder 28 minutes ago Advertiser Content From This is the title for the native ad Andrew Liszewski 31 minutes ago Adobe’s ‘computational photography’ camera app is now available for the iPad. After adding support for the iPhone 17 and its square sensor selfie camera last November, Adobe has updated its Project Indigo camera app to support the iPhone 17e and iPads with at least 6GB of RAM. But Adobe warns that “Project Indigo is not yet tuned for iPad operation” and suggests sticking with an iPhone for an “optimal experience.” Project Indigo App - App Store [App Store] Link Trump Mobile isn’t giving up just yet Dominic Preston 33 minutes ago Emma Roth 51 minutes ago Professor Layton is coming to PC and PlayStation for the first time. Arriving later this year, Professor Layton and the New World of Steam will be available on PlayStation 5 and Steam in addition to the Nintendo Switch and Switch 2. There’s still no firm release date for this puzzle-filled title just yet, but you can check out the latest trailer below. Victoria Song Apr 8 Stevie Bonifield Two hours ago France’s government is switching to Linux. France’s Interministerial Directorate for Digital Affairs (DINUM) has announced its “exit from Windows in favor of workstations running on the Linux operating system,” as XDA reports. It’s part of a broader shift away from non-European tech — in January, France’s government also launched its own videoconferencing platform to replace Teams, Zoom, and Google Meet. France's government is ditching Windows for Linux, calling US tech dependence a strategic risk [XDA] Link Emma Roth Two hours ago Prime Video’s pricier “Ultra” plan goes live today. Going ad-free on Prime Video now costs $4.99 per month, instead of $2.99. It also comes with “exclusive” access to 4K streams. Amazon Prime Video nearly doubles the price to go ad-free and stream 4K video Emma Roth Mar 13 Link Cloudflare made a WordPress for AI agents Emma Roth Two hours ago Stevie Bonifield Two hours ago Gmail’s end-to-end encryption is now available on its mobile app. Starting this week, enterprise users will be able to send encrypted messages from Gmail’s Android and iOS apps if their organization has the feature enabled. Gmail’s version of E2EE, which uses client-side encryption , has been available since last year , but is still limited to users with enterprise accounts. Gmail end-to-end encryption now available on mobile devices [Google Workspace Updates Blog] Link Dominic Preston 12:16 PM UTC Huawei’s next flagship phones are going big on gradients. The Pura 90 series will launch in China on April 20th, and the phones will be decked out in a series of colorful gradient finishes. GizmoChina has images of the full line of Pura 90 Pro and Pro Max colors, but I’m a fan of this sunset Pro Max Huawei has teased on Weibo . Gradients aren’t new for Huawei — its P20 and P30 phones had similar finishes. The fairly reliable leaker OnLeaks has shared his first CAD renders for the Galaxy Z Flip 8, via MyMobiles , and it looks… pretty much identical to the Z Flip 7 , actually. It’s apparently slimmer though, with 0.5mm shaved off when closed, which should make it fractionally more pocketable. Previous Next 1/3 The Flip 8 has the same boxy design and full cover screen as its predecessor according to this leak. After adding support for the iPhone 17 and its square sensor selfie camera last November, Adobe has updated its Project Indigo camera app to support the iPhone 17e and iPads with at least 6GB of RAM. But Adobe warns that “Project Indigo is not yet tuned for iPad operation” and suggests sticking with an iPhone for an “optimal experience.” Project Indigo App - App Store [App Store] Link Trump Mobile isn’t giving up just yet Dominic Preston Two hours ago Emma Roth Two hours ago Professor Layton is coming to PC and PlayStation for the first time. There’s still no firm release date for this puzzle-filled title just yet, but you can check out the latest trailer below. Gaby Del Valle 60 minutes ago Dominic Preston An hour ago Samsung’s Z Flip 8 slims down in new leak renders. The fairly reliable leaker OnLeaks has shared his first CAD renders for the Galaxy Z Flip 8, via MyMobiles , and it looks… pretty much identical to the Z Flip 7 , actually. It’s apparently slimmer though, with 0.5mm shaved off when closed, which should make it fractionally more pocketable. Previous Next 1/3 The Flip 8 has the same boxy design and full cover screen as its predecessor according to this leak. Image: OnLeaks / MyMobiles Link Bose’s rugged Soundlink Flex Bluetooth speaker is on sale for up to $50 off Brandon Widder An hour ago Advertiser Content From This is the title for the native ad Andrew Liszewski An hour ago Adobe’s ‘computational photography’ camera app is now available for the iPad. After adding support for the iPhone 17 and its square sensor selfie camera last November, Adobe has updated its Project Indigo camera app to support the iPhone 17e and iPads with at least 6GB of RAM. But Adobe warns that “Project Indigo is not yet tuned for iPad operation” and suggests sticking with an iPhone for an “optimal experience.” Project Indigo App - App Store [App Store] Link Trump Mobile isn’t giving up just yet Dominic Preston Two hours ago Emma Roth Two hours ago Professor Layton is coming to PC and PlayStation for the first time. Arriving later this year, Professor Layton and the New World of Steam will be available on PlayStation 5 and Steam in addition to the Nintendo Switch and Switch 2. There’s still no firm release date for this puzzle-filled title just yet, but you can check out the latest trailer below. Victoria Song Apr 8 Stevie Bonifield Two hours ago France’s government is switching to Linux. France’s Interministerial Directorate for Digital Affairs (DINUM) has announced its “exit from Windows in favor of workstations running on the Linux operating system,” as XDA reports. It’s part of a broader shift away from non-European tech — in January, France’s government also launched its own videoconferencing platform to replace Teams, Zoom, and Google Meet. France's government is ditching Windows for Linux, calling US tech dependence a strategic risk [XDA] Link Emma Roth Two hours ago Prime Video’s pricier “Ultra” plan goes live today. Going ad-free on Prime Video now costs $4.99 per month, instead of $2.99. It also comes with “exclusive” access to 4K streams. Amazon Prime Video nearly doubles the price to go ad-free and stream 4K video Emma Roth Mar 13 Link Cloudflare made a WordPress for AI agents Emma Roth 1:30 PM UTC Stevie Bonifield 1:01 PM UTC Gmail’s end-to-end encryption is now available on its mobile app. Starting this week, enterprise users will be able to send encrypted messages from Gmail’s Android and iOS apps if their organization has the feature enabled. Gmail’s version of E2EE, which uses client-side encryption , has been available since last year , but is still limited to users with enterprise accounts. Gmail end-to-end encryption now available on mobile devices [Google Workspace Updates Blog] Link Dominic Preston 12:16 PM UTC Huawei’s next flagship phones are going big on gradients. The Pura 90 series will launch in China on April 20th, and the phones will be decked out in a series of colorful gradient finishes. GizmoChina has images of the full line of Pura 90 Pro and Pro Max colors, but I’m a fan of this sunset Pro Max Huawei has teased on Weibo . Gradients aren’t new for Huawei — its P20 and P30 phones had similar finishes. Digital privacy non-profit the Electronic Frontier Foundation has announced it’s leaving X , citing a sharp decline in views for its posts. With EFF off the platform, some commenters agree with the organization’s thinking. Krankor : eff x, I agree Get the day’s best comment and more in my free newsletter, The Verge Daily . It’s apparently slimmer though, with 0.5mm shaved off when closed, which should make it fractionally more pocketable. After adding support for the iPhone 17 and its square sensor selfie camera last November, Adobe has updated its Project Indigo camera app to support the iPhone 17e and iPads with at least 6GB of RAM. Sean Hollister Apr 7 Emma Roth Two hours ago Professor Layton is coming to PC and PlayStation for the first time. Arriving later this year, Professor Layton and the New World of Steam will be available on PlayStation 5 and Steam in addition to the Nintendo Switch and Switch 2. There’s still no firm release date for this puzzle-filled title just yet, but you can check out the latest trailer below. Link Stevie Bonifield 2:10 PM UTC France’s government is switching to Linux. France’s Interministerial Directorate for Digital Affairs (DINUM) has announced its “exit from Windows in favor of workstations running on the Linux operating system,” as XDA reports. It’s part of a broader shift away from non-European tech — in January, France’s government also launched its own videoconferencing platform to replace Teams, Zoom, and Google Meet. France's government is ditching Windows for Linux, calling US tech dependence a strategic risk [XDA] Link Emma Roth 1:58 PM UTC Prime Video’s pricier “Ultra” plan goes live today. Going ad-free on Prime Video now costs $4.99 per month, instead of $2.99. It also comes with “exclusive” access to 4K streams. Amazon Prime Video nearly doubles the price to go ad-free and stream 4K video Emma Roth Mar 13 Link Cloudflare made a WordPress for AI agents Emma Roth 1:30 PM UTC Stevie Bonifield 1:01 PM UTC Gmail’s end-to-end encryption is now available on its mobile app. Starting this week, enterprise users will be able to send encrypted messages from Gmail’s Android and iOS apps if their organization has the feature enabled. Gmail’s version of E2EE, which uses client-side encryption , has been available since last year , but is still limited to users with enterprise accounts. When the four astronauts aboard Orion return to Earth tonight, they’ll rely on a heat shield that NASA has admitted is flawed. Former astronaut Dr. Charlie Camarda told the New York Times that NASA never should’ve launched Artemis II, guessing there’s a 95 percent chance it will return safely. CNN explains Artemis II’s “lofted” entry has been adjusted to try to limit the unexpected charring on the Artemis I heat shield , and National Geographic also went in-depth. YouTube , Netflix , and others will broadcast the landing starting at 6:30PM ET. If you buy something from a Verge link, Vox Media may earn a commission. See our ethics statement. When the four astronauts aboard Orion return to Earth tonight, they’ll rely on a heat shield that NASA has admitted is flawed. Former astronaut Dr. Charlie Camarda told the New York Times that NASA never should’ve launched Artemis II, guessing there’s a 95 percent chance it will return safely. CNN explains Artemis II’s “lofted” entry has been adjusted to try to limit the unexpected charring on the Artemis I heat shield , and National Geographic also went in-depth. YouTube , Netflix , and others will broadcast the landing starting at 6:30PM ET. If you buy something from a Verge link, Vox Media may earn a commission. See our ethics statement. The fairly reliable leaker OnLeaks has shared his first CAD renders for the Galaxy Z Flip 8, via MyMobiles , and it looks… pretty much identical to the Z Flip 7 , actually. It’s apparently slimmer though, with 0.5mm shaved off when closed, which should make it fractionally more pocketable. Sean Hollister Apr 7 Andrew Liszewski 3:14 PM UTC Adobe’s ‘computational photography’ camera app is now available for the iPad. After adding support for the iPhone 17 and its square sensor selfie camera last November, Adobe has updated its Project Indigo camera app to support the iPhone 17e and iPads with at least 6GB of RAM. But Adobe warns that “Project Indigo is not yet tuned for iPad operation” and suggests sticking with an iPhone for an “optimal experience.” Project Indigo App - App Store [App Store] Link Trump Mobile isn’t giving up just yet Dominic Preston 3:12 PM UTC Emma Roth 2:54 PM UTC Professor Layton is coming to PC and PlayStation for the first time. Arriving later this year, Professor Layton and the New World of Steam will be available on PlayStation 5 and Steam in addition to the Nintendo Switch and Switch 2. There’s still no firm release date for this puzzle-filled title just yet, but you can check out the latest trailer below. Link Stevie Bonifield 2:10 PM UTC France’s government is switching to Linux. France’s Interministerial Directorate for Digital Affairs (DINUM) has announced its “exit from Windows in favor of workstations running on the Linux operating system,” as XDA reports. It’s part of a broader shift away from non-European tech — in January, France’s government also launched its own videoconferencing platform to replace Teams, Zoom, and Google Meet. France's government is ditching Windows for Linux, calling US tech dependence a strategic risk [XDA] Link Emma Roth 1:58 PM UTC Prime Video’s pricier “Ultra” plan goes live today. Going ad-free on Prime Video now costs $4.99 per month, instead of $2.99. It also comes with “exclusive” access to 4K streams. Amazon Prime Video nearly doubles the price to go ad-free and stream

When the four astronauts aboard Orion return to Earth tonight, they’ll rely on a heat shield that NASA has admitted is flawed. Former astronaut Dr. Charlie Camarda told the New York Times that NASA never should’ve launched Artemis II, guessing there’s a 95 percent chance it will return safely. CNN explains Artemis II’s “lofted” entry has been adjusted to try to limit the unexpected charring on the Artemis I heat shield , and National Geographic also went in-depth.
